textByHash.b462949672cf43282a5f237ec60acd41622fb0de: I think I know of this island. Ungael, we call it. It is a dark place, guarded by a deadly beast. Some of our finest warriors tried to take the beast down, but to no success.
textByHash.b64c87ebd38e8221005a4e80a979194861a690d4: Do you intend to slay this beast for yourself?
textByHash.859d1e0a17f53575f7d65b44e41965139400d5f8: This is no easy task, , but glory awaits you if you succeed.
textByHash.6c44bb88192cc304cc1ec325279365862d2464a7: Go see Torfinn on the docks. He can take you to Ungael. Good luck.
textByHash.66e767fcb34b128280818979a6b475f299c2be6f: He should do. Just tell him I sent you.
textByHash.c3992a35c297ccb8ff1b37e97d05a32d681bf1d7: Greetings. Have you destroyed that beast yet?
textByHash.3a62f8247bf418868229612d824c1b0720017479: Well if you speak to Torfinn on the docks he will take you to Ungael. Good luck.
textByHash.3b47a0cb0b51d5eae5c75dbc4ea47545aab014d1: Greetings. Have you seen those beasts flying towards Ungael? Might be best to stay clear of that place for a while.
textByHash.95857b29e57ffd148b194b6edc985fd895df60c2: Let me guess, you intend to take these beasts on?
textByHash.d8ddff19d0568daa29d9c9af30fedec36cf3712d: You are a true warrior, , though I fear this may be too much even for you. You are heavily outnumbered.
textByHash.49d3ca492f809b441a15b93b07023a7784c8ff9d: Fight alongside outerlanders? That is not typically our way.
textByHash.6c9eb678738b8aded39c4825e75a1343c2122e1b: Well let it not be said that the Fremennik turned away from a good fight. You have our support. Maybe we'll show your outerlander friends a thing or two!
textByHash.b47a4e3cf0a5af6ec56d4eba0d3e33d5f24667f9: The council won't be happy. I'll tell them we're doing it to show your outerlander friends how to fight. That should convince them.
textByHash.43ac403bdf9e20f83893b69f965d220313723014: Pah! Outerlanders and their meetings. I will attend, but this better not take long. We have dragons to kill!
textByHash.22f3847b256a8405bbba033d954b81a7e27bc3b0: I shall be heading there soon.
textByHash.c91be1bfddd4f5c853c61753567a1af3e2710d33: Let me guess, you used your magic to work that one out?
textByHash.6f31ebd3a43301b465234fafbc066a257dadb3e5: The dragons are amassing at Ungael. We should take the fight to them and destroy the danger before it leaves that island!
textByHash.2a9012b689ef9be42b8540304b6e45f53644ccc2: You should join us for this fight. Torfinn will take you to the fleet when you are ready.
textByHash.767afd04a1d955749d2fc27e2fefb5f894afb322: A Seal of Passage? Whatever for?
textByHash.253871be2274447a59c04fd55eaab772de06e32c: Ah , many have tried, but The Moon Clan are by no means a sharing group of people. They do everything by magic, something we never use. But they are so secretive about it. If only they would share, we
textByHash.91ea4f70da269081fd3a1d93aaca75078017402a: wouldn't have to kill them all the time!
textByHash.de543fe3f35e865af0cb8bd717f742ce8286dd27: Yes, but you have no room to carry it. You should have thought of that before!
textByHash.b0cb6faef7bf843b2dc30bf6777dcf37f9e554f4: If you are to bring peace to our two warring clans, then perhaps it is best that you do...
textByHash.a8dfcbcf1e3cea8dd090d13ddea559f58b0f34b8: Then you had better take a replacement and be less careless in future.
textByHash.ce169316902d4d4878225a803aeb2b7ec466fd77: Ah. How goes the peace talks with the accursed Moon Clan?
textByHash.503530ac38117bb9c1f5f4f344833621fecf23e7: I see. Well, I don't hold out for much hope!
textByHash.12bad6bce181d359c594761aa0c443006434dfc2: Well if it isn't . I've heard of your exploits in the Lunar Isles!
textByHash.c9ab76157b595a0874e10939186112c86e21ca14: Thank you so very much, I think we can really make progress now.
textByHash.a98427e8fab5c258d9264d841bab2d2756c3ab8a: Seal of Passage, well I suppose so.
textByHash.5fae39b5a95b620f5e373db0700824aeab1335f6: Very unusual, outerlander, but we have not seen him yet.
textByHash.a0f00e6f58473ca4e560475cc46161c613b9f229: What? He's not much of a warrior, is he?
textByHash.6b73feead54168bd7fdd03b6f2b0d4c821777bb3: I will meet with this man on one condition, outerlander.
textByHash.774c35aaa02315e85f6ec017880f3d5173a9886a: When their group split off from Rellekka, they stole one of the ancient relics of the Fremennik. Our ancient rock.
textByHash.a2a3780986f883f79b095b3acebe6e2e859fbb4b: Amusing, , but as a Fremennik yourself you know better than that.
textByHash.879053ea7db246e8eb2141aa94f7965808afe8cb: No, no, no. Little do you understand our ways, outerlander.
textByHash.80a956f152f657f15fe9ccc7b94d4bf3963cb6d3: The rock was just that, a rock. It had no special properties and was in essence no different from any other rock.
textByHash.b85af8e4fe9b6483aef3c54d782cb9379ae040f4: It symbolised our refusal to use the runestones for ourselves, instead relying on our own abilities.
textByHash.bab137a67f0984b731d0339b222e8069f54a4be7: The people you speak of took the stone with them when they left for the mountains. They claimed their way of living was more pure and closer to nature.
textByHash.e6b19b275e0d80265343cf6381cad5792400828e: Maybe, but where's the harm in a bit of feasting from time to time?
textByHash.db3e6e99d29ce3d4cea3d97d39007943cad91330: In any case, before the council and I meet with anyone from their camp, they will have to give our rock back.
textByHash.801ea9d82e0aae3f34f96bfc4c0114f29274f54b: Like I said, we don't want anything to do with them until they give back our ancient rock.
textByHash.26eb7502c0c4274b2911f1d51f8fd86a0d051643: This rock seems a lot smaller than our bard made it out to be.
textByHash.5588b580a27ac475209ac64e5b8342121974116d: You truly have the Fremennik spirit in you,
textByHash.7467d163271816858834ace784a0a7325a4fe5d3: You are unusually insightful for an outerlander!
textByHash.9d1e755153a6ee6eea89345c8c80f8b6417db679: Yes, we are satisfied with sharing the rock. I'll meet with this representative and will inform the council to join us here in the longhall.
textByHash.5a1a56acb72daf5684aa39c079b20b05dbd7e443: Although I see that Manni is already here.
textByHash.eb006a2ddaa30676c0249261c5af9e062c9b0e94: As a matter of fact , I do have a quest for you. Are you interested?
textByHash.db4383588d105b83a9ef8c2b3a3a4c7f1073bce6: I'm a fan of your enthusiasm, , but-
textByHash.78c645d944e51e6b64a6063aeb701bc6d8b13b42: Nothing quite that fancy I'm afraid. I just need you to investigate a minor disturbance.
textByHash.c0bf1f0839a312042e90e6dc4ef7a34c9aa46569: Freygerd and Reeso run the sandpit a little north of here. They mentioned they heard some rustling by their hut last night and wanted an official to investigate.
textByHash.ef22845c98b3184aa90735379c85d325d830ba27: You've proven yourself more than worthy of our people's trust.
textByHash.8ccf0d5ed7895d969ec9c0ea187354b0754d9c21: Very well. Return when you wish to help.
textByHash.b86ce4b9428c11d8a879a99c8c134175cf5f0c8f: No rush. I'm sure it's nothing to worry about. Just talk to Freygerd and Reeso at the sandpit when you're ready.
textByHash.81ff308a8317a7bc588c52aa0e06abfedda63346: , I'm glad you've returned! This letter you have found is very worrying and requires our utmost attention.
textByHash.c3c327e00f3b69683756665efa0d3e6fc3868780: Never did we think this day would come.
textByHash.bccb40484acff30a8a5b97cf8708ab4ec46595f9: So many lives lost. So many sacrifices.
textByHash.5e602d097e0b7c6faac661eb2bffdb64eac84229: But the Fremennik people shall not bow to the Jormungand again!
textByHash.f20cd062bc666452f38c17edbab221fd6f7be7c7: , please read the letter and talk to me afterwards with any questions you may have.
textByHash.8c47f27b3c99b46fbe19cf96a4afb6b0407e7cab: While they may not show it, basilisks are much smarter than they seem. Many years ago, a great basilisk army enslaved our people and took our home.
textByHash.14d58cdec8088a557081e4514481d4c46b6dcb4d: The Jormungand, sometimes known as The Serpent King, was the leader of this army. Along with his three generals, Typhor, Vritra and Bakuna, he brought a terror to these lands unlike any other.
textByHash.056fedeb47eee0c075dbbc3d8196698f30f240ed: If it hadn't been for the bravery of a great hero, we might never have been freed.
textByHash.5c377454c4cca1faecb7092b61ecf7ab69912b71: Yes. He was known as V. He stood up to the Serpent King and confronted him on the Island of Stone. It is said that the two of them fought for days, but in the end, V stood victorious.
textByHash.fc8bb952d0517ab4b590f02eb9f3d36369fa5ebf: Well that depends who you ask. The bards wrote a great song about V's victory. They called it the Ballad of the Basilisk, and it claims V killed the Jormungand.
textByHash.147b480de6e850e7e467b69b103738cfb9783dce: Well... bards are known to bend the truth a bit, especially if it makes a song sound better. In reality, V sealed the Serpent King in a magical prison on the island, where he remains to this day.
textByHash.991444b3b053cc81da09d3126bb4ec9f2a2e1eaa: V was different. Stories tell of how he unlocked the potential of magic with the single touch of a stone. In that moment, he became more than just a man.
textByHash.4df8d088ea05f5210880a4a42844e62b49e48384: It's not a distinction the Fremennik particularly care for. To us, he has always been a hero to be respected, not a god to be worshipped. They say he felt the same way.
textByHash.3db86938cfa81848446746a632cd33464d78f751: Exactly. Now the Moon Clan on the other hand, did not earn that right. Their use of magic is an insult to our people.
textByHash.2890a25788b31be43d985b4e4b3b6c13f3ebf5a8: Our army isn't large enough to hold back the forces of the Serpent King.
textByHash.f03a7afbf17e822514cf6e24f3dd3fe2a38b170e: , you're strong. But you're no army.
textByHash.c982f8ead083588a29bbc832667f502d58f74a95: Well I won't deny there's been a little issue but and I will soon have things back under control.
textByHash.498cf0bceed637aa31fb03f13d3da08da01d05b9: is no outerlander! He passed our trials and is a Fremennik, just like you and I.
textByHash.8ba0e76af51c973cfa187d9462e0b2ede9065369: This is outrageous! is one of us!
textByHash.16fd89f7f84e0407d9f0db88b51094011add010c: Exile, you must leave our province. Our people will no longer see you as their brother.
textByHash.ed7de9ca19ef2a53fcede1fc988efab3eebb9186: Leave at once. Do not attempt to interact with myself or my people.
textByHash.1a79a61243e9d217a5343e8a1d92c5f4b149f76f: Begone exile, you are a Fremennik no more.
textByHash.8a3c05ef344582bbc1d6b00c2450014936de7e80: Indeed. I'm sorry . The reality is that many on the council did not take kindly to you becoming one of us. They've been looking for a good excuse to exile you ever since you arrived.
textByHash.fb7d99ed9b73a0d9569f3a431da820a6656fceb0: Well... not quite. Seven of the twelve council members gave you their vote, but you never had the support of the other five. They're the ones who've been pushing for your exile.
textByHash.8f002bb1f1d60a346d4fad7373d2c162ff190ba8: It would seem so. Try not to judge them too harshly though, they only had what was best for the province in mind. This basilisk situation has a lot of people scared.
textByHash.d22a88c66faf9987829ce5f13bf2f07860ffa21f: Ah, I see you are not familiar with the End of Things.
textByHash.854fa8c4753812b4712e0009eecbe92e86d94b45: Many years ago, before the time of V, it was foretold that this world will end in a great battle. They called this battle Ragnarok, or more commonly, the End of Things.
textByHash.6dd937bbf3357339b2de7a478a3d9590899937bd: The prophecies say that the battle will begin after the Serpent King is freed. So you see, it is not the basilisks people fear, it is what comes after.
textByHash.bd7e8e7776dac51166e7d2c92775f9436a31542d: Well, , that's why I asked you to meet me out here. If you are to help us put a stop to this threat, you'll need to regain the trust of the council. And I think I know just how you can do that.
textByHash.13e9b15e98079a69b5cd4dae3ed4dbcffef476eb: I think the best possible way to show your worth to them is by being the person that restores V's Shield.
textByHash.3757f819eeebea36088d634ea78886fcc4d86059: The shield used by V when he defeated the Serpent King. It is legendary amongst our people. If we are to have any chance of stopping this threat, we will need to remake it.
textByHash.fb846d08e767b3e421a7b74580de816e98aecf3e: There's a few things I know about the shield, though other things are lost from stories past.
textByHash.a0f287cf8fa80898922c1f9220d612b43ba34d52: I believe some members of the Moon Clan might know the details I don't, so your diplomatic relations should come in handy.
textByHash.91366d90ae2c1e72dc67e34f0f1ff92feddd23dd: Keep in mind, now that you've been exiled, most people in the province will be unlikely to talk to you.
textByHash.edccb4b1de103e8dc53fbeb5e6ec2930f7841050: Stories tell of how V's Shield was made using four components.
textByHash.cc1df6a5e7a3db7fb236a274cbb29823292b0e21: First, the base of the shield. This was a traditional Fremennik Shield, like the ones our warriors use.
textByHash.c06c8b0b83678cad1392cec99edb68beb1a02cff: Next, the Lunar Glass. This was apparently created by a great Moon Clan mystic and was used to reflect the Serpent King's gaze.
textByHash.ede68281f886996325db1894134e724702a73864: Then you have the Polishing Rock. This was needed to shine the Lunar Glass. This rock was heated softly in a boiling spring.
textByHash.dc811520320721d43aef3e74d873c591d5f53e42: Finally, V's Sigil. This sigil was crafted by the Moon Clan, using a unique metal known only to them.
textByHash.948f4a999610f936ade003d96c9c47d3100144c5: Fremennik Shields are only given to warriors of the province.
textByHash.a8f9225767fb0246f405341ca155c405dd22dfd4: No, this shield is different. I'd recommend heading to Waterbirth Island. The daggermouths within the dungeon will likely have some from warriors they've sent to Valhalla.
textByHash.db189ac30b2373e5c1826b361e6ba2c9b995df2e: You could slay them and reclaim a shield from their corpses.
textByHash.f3f4f3c5d1e886fa466578b62fe2269f2910b5af: There's a warrior down there known as Bardur, gathering many a daggermouth hide and saving Fremennik Shields.
textByHash.0451343c05185a7a5cc85d5e29e793ef45322dde: If you have no luck finding a shield, perhaps you could charm him to give one to you?
textByHash.afdf8b31c7db80fba1defdbe169234417bed17f6: Do you have any more questions about V's Shield?
textByHash.cf16392726690b6873c54f176a07e49eab50dc99: I'm not sure I'm afraid. I imagine someone from the Moon Clan would likely have more knowledge on the matter.
textByHash.abe2204168c8179d59c9c24798796631f5751bf7: Well firstly, a rock would probably help...
textByHash.31533c27e61cf6899fd5f453f5d6fc643aa7790d: Convenient. As for the matter of heating the rock, there's some hot springs on the mountain to the east that should be warm enough.
textByHash.12dae7d8f44b1359d49ee67d533b0cd6953c1876: Use something to lower the rock into one of the geysers there, and you should be good to go.
textByHash.83d39739ce7b0b931ce145a19505beac78f25c63: V's Sigil is, well... in the shape of a V.
textByHash.211a1c2fdaf993876b585edcee320a778dd5fe7b: Well, one side of the sigil is shaped like a basilisk. Spines down its back, and maw wide like a gate.
textByHash.9b1965bdb5762e9d8b53cb851b0caba873c37c99: Nothing fancy. Just the other side of the letter V.
textByHash.594134a77e4ef5b2f359a9eadb593bb6b970b06a: The stories tell of a cave on Lunar Isle where V first gained his power. Apparently it contains a unique metal used only by the Moon Clan.
textByHash.9f725f11c81aa3de84b0e6c2f141fc477bc6175d: I'd say three bars of this metal should be enough. Take them to an anvil and you should be able to make the sigil.
textByHash.0eb3f73e44ca1242f8ce88d14b77b905c5d5172e: Once you have made the sigil, you'll need to have it enchanted. This was apparently done by a powerful spirit.
textByHash.9750ffd53580af6916b9fad6c2a6ab0a544e3bf9: The stories didn't specify, though I would wager the Fossegrimen might be able to help.
textByHash.3143ff67773413a3c732e5bc422094151787f09f: Well what are you waiting for? Get it made and we'll have everything we need to put an end to your exile.
textByHash.0c1753166f1688a0af478a83233b997cd1e1ab92: That's certainly one way to put it... Perhaps you should go and get it?
textByHash.b1916db9d7fde6a946c1eb2a606cb1203342dfca: ... This is incredible! It's as if you forged the shield from a picture. It's exactly how I imagined it from the stories!
textByHash.42ecaaa1e43705abeca27e7b719b36ec841ebccf: I hope so . I do suspect some of them may never respect you though.
textByHash.74ff8db929fde8176a2d822600cd68dbc215bbe9: The shield. It requires one last thing.
textByHash.8ff11e94e84298c93a46f4596623d978f47ad6d3: Return to me with two beers. No... Two kegs of beer! Quickly! There's not much time!
textByHash.96002817361c8bd7649bc02937c771d20a3084bb: Have you got the two kegs of beer?
textByHash.447db01d110984926cbba781805af91c56121f0f: I'm litchrally abousht to go shtand uhp to the enshtire [cough] counshil for yooo...! I need thish!
textByHash.caa6d60e254e103eb4c14eccce723efb6228265d: You're a bigger alcoholic than I am! Hand them over.
textByHash.ca31eac88587ce1f94311cd54c35ecf39fffa254: We Fremennik know how to handle a drink. Anyway, I have some good news for you.
textByHash.88a6b8458d4403045c12c0fb62be666220fa0084: Following your successful recreation of V's Shield, the council have agreed to end your exile. I hearby declare you a Fremennik again!
textByHash.64197e4b4b9845a31f6a1dee6a797831b7027bce: Not only that, but we would like you to be the one to carry V's legendary shield into battle.
textByHash.7a1c3f4ecdb92dd659f5c63cdcd34cd0682555a4: So you should be . V was our greatest hero. There is no greater honour than to bear his shield in battle.
textByHash.4094657d35f165b26e62eef9bc87ffadde978102: The basilisks intend to attack the town so they can keep us distracted while they free the Jormungand. Luckily for us, we know about this.
textByHash.0facfca7bad863ce59dec00f3de733849cdb92c7: Once the attack begins, the majority of our forces will stay here to hold the town. While they do, you and I will journey to the Island of Stone.
textByHash.f6a7d5953c55e3ffdab05f8bd32496e24b3d8519: There, we will ensure that the prison of the Jormungand remains sealed.
textByHash.0b912af150543a33afd29f94d9c6d7b9e60eccae: They've walked right through our defences!
textByHash.abe4811831f14d8889bbeaaa7d6cbf37e816842f: Get to the boat. It's time for us to go!
textByHash.6c318966cd4fae2849fa739b094ea5d52edf8083: This isn't good . The prison has already been breached.
textByHash.5e7d59ea012584b79abb758996575af376b0451e: Not quite, but we'll have to act fast. If the Jormungand manages to leave the prison, the End of Things will be upon us.
textByHash.a58dc8111f04187cb7cce4f614cc84941def69dd: It looks like the basilisks resealed the door behind them. We'll have to get through it.
textByHash.2be17e326047048dfd2bbb3c5feafe8f36aed4db: From what I can tell, the door uses a combination lock. You'll need to get the right combination to unlock it. Too many wrong attempts will reset the lock though.
textByHash.990848c6b4199b7da3137b43a85832f2f2ba607b: Hurry . We can't afford to waste any time.
textByHash.b76ed08b63870373702ff6383cef66dcaf202201: Have you opened the door yet?
textByHash.28d506332386f42d06258ec8bdd77660a18db340: Hurry . We need to get inside before it's too late.
textByHash.1b35e8bc0564afc6aed94b0c12ea7bc3b33fbfef: Good work . Let's get inside and see what's going on.
textByHash.343ee40a535dfaa6e30ede2cb4c0a9df81da83b6: Let's get inside and see what's going on.
textByHash.509043ff8f61a1cc34859dfc23f6f44a41e92a2e: With me ! Let's kill these foul beasts!
textByHash.85e42011cb2bc84d16caaef1e9d406c2cb2ee41a: This isn't over yet , the Jormungand is weak from his imprisonment! We can still stop him!
textByHash.0f9ba9f9ff59e87a6e095ac5320e7e12319eb7bd: You will not defeat us this day! Come !
textByHash.dd76c687bd8bd5280479f9e6bf6b2872760305c5: The stone is weak! You can break it!
textByHash.5f5d4eefec2be90a92da8c5d8e561f75f7ea2542: Hurry ! We need to get inside and stop those basilisks!
textByHash.6f8d07af2f591fc0a0bdcc1bfd9a3a03a98ca03e: Wait ! I have some things for you!
textByHash.685d1ce5f542e74ba08f29a1f0d14fef80d11330: I do have some of your items here . Do try to be careful with them.
textByHash.5ed88cc30078df46119d0dc44ab26f7b64621864: I'm afraid I don't have anything for you . If I did have any of your items, but you died before collecting them, they'll now be lost.
textByHash.de256918605a0913d6504b97ccfbd49d2a9eb03f: Ah, nothing like a glorious fight to make you feel alive!
textByHash.5422fc80563021272e5f7bd06f7ad114cece0c30: Now? We drink of course! The Jormungand has been defeated and the End of Things prevented!
textByHash.afb243c35efdedca307b75717ea949d398571b52: You worry too much . If the end had begun, we'd have noticed by now. Come! Let us go and celebrate!
textByHash.0a7864daf62a8b869331d44d443d7143a72bd896: But it wouldn't have been possible without !
textByHash.679d2ce351552a0e8f284005a67dcce47fe1274e: This place? Why, this is Rellekka! Homeland of all Fremennik! I do not recognise your face outerlander; Where do you come from?
textByHash.99903eb29b1dba772857b4ec3bbd4328dab230b9: Hmmm... I will not press the issue then outerlander. How may my tribe and I help you?
textByHash.d8fea3b4a87f0887d3a53034db40e3b03f964589: Quests, you say outerlander? Well, I would not call it a quest as such, but if you are brave of heart and strong of body, perhaps...
textByHash.a3e2d399e52fb374c3e578514e301713916e9c62: No, you would not be interested. Forget I said anything, outerlander.
textByHash.f7882d63215d8d24a0b00e99c8fa1d0d10794472: You would? These are unusual sentiments to hear from an outerlander! My suggestion was going to be that if you crave adventure and battle,
textByHash.21a3c79c71d19c55d8de464daa0e6eab8c9c8cea: and your heart sings for glory, then perhaps you would be interested in joining our clan, and becoming a Fremennik yourself?
textByHash.f6f4459bfb3db2f7f0df0ff561992ad72266c727: Well, there are two ways to become a member of our clan and call yourself a Fremennik: be born a Fremennik, or be voted in by our council of elders.
textByHash.e4778a3e51b0739c01cc80eaf2a9cd35fa1bc427: Well, that I cannot answer myself. You will need to speak to each of them and see what they require of you as proof of your dedication.
textByHash.d1d7d80cb0f2bf5f0bf0fb500e8822c516d3ed0e: There are twelve council members around this village; you will need to gain a majority vote of at least seven councillors in your favour.
textByHash.0303a6ded09e2cc297ea66bc44e749b6a92c7f5a: So what say you? Give me the word, and I will tell all of my tribe of your intentions, be they yea or nay.
textByHash.f721654d0d6ac79485afbee707c56374b63dfdf9: As I say outerlander, you must find and speak to the twelve members of the council of elders, and see what tasks they might set you.
textByHash.163c8748fa1dd6e728b4bfe5167bc490d2ec815f: If you can gain the support of seven of the twelve, then you will be accepted as one of us without question.
textByHash.0285dbceee8374dbcb7e8511b1fbdbd1e099f036: I am sorry to say that I expected such a response from an outerlander. I thought perhaps you might have been different to all the others...
textByHash.ac0e8e20369bf9b18fef5e1f717b6ca83c01ad25: It is as I thought outerlander. Your kind care nothing for the Fremennik, and we feel the same about you. This is the way of the world.
textByHash.32bd495227a1943df60e910f323124b585f56cd1: Do not take it personally, outerlander! We are a simple people, and it is our experience that keeping ourselves to ourselves is best. This is why speaking to outerlanders is forbidden.
textByHash.92cbada57dad1d2c01265f1c659036f7c9fda765: We do not wish to enter war with the outerlanders and their strange magics, so we limit all unauthorised communication.
textByHash.e0b8d3e1c2ddc7a5dda552efcebbd9b2ba3159cf: Ah, this is because I am the chieftain. I am the one who authorises contact. You will not find many of my tribe so forthcoming with you, as I.
textByHash.55010afe87d01017b8d495f7161a41ea66ad71ac: Well, there is one way... but I doubt it is of any interest to you.
textByHash.8a6295936a9dcf8ced9df97804654097287b3489: Greetings again outerlander! How goes your attempts to gain votes with the council of elders?
textByHash.74ae5249d3f81ca205dffaaf851eec2453e1173b: A reduction on sales taxes? Why, I am the only one in the Fremennik who may authorise such a thing. What does an outerlander want with that?
textByHash.1bc4af82b5a417f020b2c6df968a319e6017424f: Hmmm. Interesting. Your trials seem to be very different to those I took as a young lad. Well, I am not adverse in principle to giving a slight tax break to our shops.
textByHash.dacb609ebea2b7d262163a061251c2cfce15d85f: There will of course be a shortfall in the tribe's income, that will need to be made up for elsewhere, however.
textByHash.767db17496fe1263b8f3857f378e3a8d33e2e2c7: How about this. For many years Sigli has been the only one in the tribe who knows the locations of the best hunting grounds where game is easiest to catch.
textByHash.1af89b1204af41dc5f5b0958fbbbfde150a904d0: If you can persuade him to let the entire tribe know these hunting grounds, then we can increase productivity within the tribe, and any shortfall caused
textByHash.dc3fb6fa5dac3459f1eb9215d008eb3e4e08a876: by lowering sales taxes will be covered. I think this is a more than fair arrangement to make, don't you?
textByHash.bd169c8d21723a27c92a1e6234b171e2e6899e7a: Speak to Sigli then, and you may have my promise to reduce our sales taxes. And best of luck with the rest of your trials.
textByHash.752001abf21cfd155a626134318638543c5566b4: Excellent work outerlander! And so quickly, too! Here, you may take my financial report promising reduced sales taxes on all goods.
textByHash.e9d1689f6d83658b4a7a2e1c90186332f7372e28: Not at all outerlander; now that we have Sigli's map we can increase the amount of hunts we run, and make up any shortfall that way.
textByHash.7a87c80c54967139f20d53342e40c0c33899501d: Hmmm... well that is certainly a good start I would say. Keep up the good work!
textByHash.b5081be82bb702dff5e05e649ec55b44b7462c48: Hmmm... you are doing very well so far, outerlander. Keep up the good work!
textByHash.e287f02d9a7c00d22eb2b7784613269273ff4187: Remember: You need to get at least seven council votes to be accepted as a member of the Fremennik.
textByHash.a7c738300d012d79718f09fe603d6cb2dca3ad77: If you need any help with your trials, I suggest you speak to Askeladden. He is currently doing his own trials of manhood to become a true Fremennik.
textByHash.3c76c2fcd1f63819ea6389af7127273725c8ba73: I know outerlander, for I have been closely monitoring your progress so far!
textByHash.b6dd048e587921a388b3ab5d2743630481728820: Then let us put the formality aside, and let me personally welcome you into the Fremennik! May you bring us honour!
textByHash.5c77b51782370f6307ec78c5b0b3f165656a4aa5: From this day onward, you are outerlander no more! In honour of your acceptance into the Fremennik You gain a new name to be known as. You will now be called .
textByHash.d526aa4932f9c2baffa815c0f2976442e3d6a599: Do not take it as an insult, outerlander. For us, this town is the center of all. Anyone not born here, or that we do not know and trust is an outerlander.
textByHash.4cb342561ff1c13ac63d208455c873f5451de492: Aye, but that is an outerlander word. We do not judge someones ways by their name. It simply means one from the outerlands, or one not from Rellekka.
textByHash.f5dfa2a128c63b4b21a7eceef9564e61cacd1be8: I know our customs are foreign to you; perhaps you would like me to explain something else?
textByHash.c062499ac4feb664387bbc220a13d5d36a44386a: We will have no part in the squabbles of outerlander tribes. Their magics are strange and deadly to us, as we learnt long ago.
textByHash.74f60bcfb44ab74d22225f7680888ed5fb311dc4: If this is all that you came here for, then you have had a wasted journey. If you are looking for something else, then speak now for my patience dwindles.
textByHash.fc707471a98a1e327ae03f0819dd8afd5ac0ed06: It is the name we call ourselves. Outerlanders tend to call us 'Barbarian', but they do not understand our ways...
textByHash.133e12fb69b6fe9898fcf2e3be8fa8a03b41e576: Do not mock me outerlander; this helm has saved my life more times than I care to recall right now.
textByHash.2559c6b589bea08ed06107b5ee79a2507099a264: It went surprisingly well! We are at least at the point where we will freely acknowledge each other's existence now.
textByHash.ec123c86dd35e294854d950d33df25194f23c24c: You are definitely a great asset to the Fremennik!
textByHash.43f83f6336ea7f06473a3458c45360f87ad32840: I don't think you have . I suggest you try looking for it again.
textByHash.898a9ec2f1ab32aa595fabefc1211d8470174f99: You really should be more careful . Luckily for you I found it again but you'll have to pay to get it back.
textByHash.320227267e552cc0f5cb9f1ed9e56350c08d5e29: 50,000 coins. Consider it a lesson in looking after things better.
textByHash.175495986f1586903d41618267cfca334dcf58a1: Here you go . Use it well.
textByHash.9ba454800f66860ec93eeec5f27f138ea20a41f4: Fair enough. I'll be here if you change your mind.
textByHash.b7e7fa6e473c59076f038580dc543e54ad3848f2: Well come back when you do.
textByHash.54a4c7690cc1c8762b4730a09a7a1e9db201171d: Hello again brother ! I hope your travels have brought you wealth and joy! What compels you to visit me on this day?
textByHash.cdb742e8c113eb514f493635d62944e92ad0133f: Hello again sister ! I hope your travels have brought you wealth and joy! What compels you to visit me on this day?
textByHash.84a264da58c213c628307c4a0feed94fabf8a087: Well met, brother . It is always good to see your face in glorious Rellekka once more!
textByHash.9964a47c6cc4177fe099609d04a931a591653431: Well met, sister . It is always good to see your face in glorious Rellekka once more!
textByHash.7223f1d49b12eedf2fe7deaa980d98b9d200c482: Not at the moment . Rest assured, should your services to the Fremennik be required, I will call upon you.
textByHash.c58a0162d224df60275fa916d93d4ea4b3dd650a: Aye, that it is brother . Should you desire one of your own, you should go to Skulgrimen's shop and see what they have available!
textByHash.9f8cc3244574cb01096930a51420d59bb61adb84: Aye, that it is sister . Should you desire one of your own, you should go to Skulgrimen's shop and see what they have available!
textByHash.f7826e34592d8649b909244315a9d39112abc34a: Why, of course ! Do not say 'your people' like that, for you are now a Fremennik yourself! What did you want to hear of?
textByHash.34710ec99477c7a82094cd9eeaf8efa2332f6b86: Ah... the deathless one... We do not even know if Koschei is his name, when we found him he had no memory, he was simply repeating that single word.
textByHash.790d5d9f005129d74670257060c2a3c4e2d6dfdb: Whatever happened to him must have rattled his soul so hard that his memory ran away from him, and he has not yet found it's hiding place.
textByHash.48178cc96f9c02e239aac4987a5513e533cf95ad: Well, it was a raiding party like any other. We had just struck one of the smaller northern islands to harvest supplies by combat, as our laws allow,
textByHash.087eaaed922aeab2c69b978958b89bd2e19917d4: when we saw a figure in the icey waters. As we were at least a 3 week sail from any port, and saw no other ships nearby, he must have been there some time.
textByHash.7a575581592ad139225ae7d2925539afca318346: We could not leave someone to the cold death of the ocean, even an outerlander, so we fished him aboard. He muttered continually, but his words made no sense.
textByHash.cd2edd81d142995eba0e9062a8a96d29790532d8: He had no clothes or possessions with him, and seemed to have been burnt somehow before he had landed in the water.
textByHash.13dd8e7e77165a0dab5363df96950f2f6b2f1622: I cannot help but wonder what burnt him, for as you may have seen, he is incredibly powerful and resistant to damage by any means I know of!
textByHash.76541bf374156d9e134e9c0290b6c9100aa150ed: Anyway, we decided to bring him back to Rellekka, for he did not seem of good health or sound mind, and simply repeated the word Koschei over and over.
textByHash.71a12ae3f1986733828f172fecbaeecdf3e8a2a0: Yrsa tended to him for many weeks, until one day it was as though a new soul had entered his burnt and broken body.
textByHash.d86cd0d12f8e1cd8c0438d186acf3d92733a8448: He suddenly left her house one day, fully healed, and able to speak to us as though nothing had happened to him.
textByHash.5fdbed84ef9d36a3ccab9e1c63ec01e163b3a563: He still had no recollection of how he had come to be in the icey ocean, or of who he was, or where he had come from, but this did not matter to us or him.
textByHash.3e5a1c65538518b53f89908a79a4130c5a401ee3: He took his trials of adulthood, as you did, and has been a boon to our clan ever since. Someday I fear he may regain his memory, and leave us for his past...
textByHash.47db16e5de80da10f7f4676c874456e6d7ed04f7: But I will always respect any decision he decides to make should that day ever come upon us. Until then, he is more than welcome to stay here.
textByHash.5d0ec68e501744bbad890ee373ba3770d60b6f73: So. Was there anything more you wished to hear?
textByHash.3877fee9bca8890749ada2d2acb662905acd1e4d: Well, I think the best thing for you would be to go to the docks and ask to join one of our longboats on a journey.
textByHash.5a974df9af655edb97be12b705f8e1b11de872e5: My affairs are concentrated mostly on Rellekka, so my knowledge is not great, but I will happily share what I do know with you .
textByHash.55994af6cbc414ee7889717fd66860bc5d16a59b: North of this town the atmosphere becomes bitterly cold. There are a number of small inhabited islands that we have sea routes with.
textByHash.9029827f3e7cfab082d4812ad4f2746ac1d88b8b: The largest of these is possibly Miscellania, although I hear that there has been some recent incident with their monarch, but I don't know the details.
textByHash.61245be0bfac39b452f3a0cd8ffd19227ddbea80: There is also of course the island of the moon clan, who change with the tides. They are our bitterest enemies, and we are currently at war with them.
textByHash.2a20066c29e5d83b3e840c5b7963c06edd2f3792: They are an evil people, equally cursed and blessed by the magic in their blood. Beyond those islands, there is the large frozen wasteland we call Acheron.
textByHash.d0ca663a9019557502163076ea7835d14a41ce2e: It is an inhospitable land, and you will need an agile and sturdy body just to keep alive in it's perils.
textByHash.b8101fe5ddb9fea8a68874c77bab21de0d453a84: As I say, my knowledge outside of this town is rather limited, and I know no more than this. Was there anything else you wished to hear tell of?
textByHash.c8fc023f5e97c619a8fc65a27eb0899eaef5bba6: Ah, now that is something I know a great deal about. Believe it or not, all outerlanders were once orginally Fremenniks.
textByHash.1022e48e12c8005d9a05d776e7cc3a729da4d6a9: When first man came to these lands all were Fremenniks, and followed our ways. We lived a happy life, and never settled in one place for long.
textByHash.0b864ff1674e52a8f660ea97f33b0930113d4147: We travelled by boat along the coastlines, never taking more from the land than could be regrown by the same time in the following year.
textByHash.52107d28a8ffa7f1a1d9f5a6bbbdba8de44497b0: However, many moons past, some of our tribesmen were weary of constantly travelling the lands, and decided to build themselves permanent homes.
textByHash.abaac8099fc9bf0892010cb5ea7ee51a3c9f78fc: Unfortunately, the other races that had also migrated here did not like this. They waged war against us at every opportunity.
textByHash.53f5e2e6c9df2e8e19395c764cd30b10cb141c10: We were driven mostly to the coastlines, where we could escape by boat when attacked. Remember, at this time we had no real way to defend against constant attack.
textByHash.ccb4d35b9fd691aac278a52a83c2440e8f8bd0d6: Then one day one of our Seers, whose name has been lost in the telling of this tale, discovered a cave full of strange rocks.
textByHash.fe13788ed2e59bd4ea63c400c4994e1b120c9918: This rock would never be consumed, no matter how much we took, and sparkled at its peak, and broke off in small and regular sized chunks when mined.
textByHash.b59eb776d0d02bd4c2cf1ad588f4eb8d341541b3: Stranger than this, we found that these rocks would become filled with power when taken to certain places across this land!
textByHash.caeb7411e4bc8d28ea5c79ff2b3bdd5ca6f3459e: There was a terrible argument in our tribe! Some said that we should take as many of these rocks as we could, and use them in defending against our enemies attacks!
textByHash.b4319338474d7e163fbcb790bf4830a2f74eedaa: Others said that we had found a place that belonged only to the gods, and that should we steal what was not ours we would find only torment and misery.
textByHash.b89c10d1250c0cf0781f72a2425a77ad8c995fd2: We voted on this, and decided that it was in the best interests of the tribe to leave these strange rocks where they lay, for the gods can be spiteful and cruel,
textByHash.b1707e38844fb583163bffbf0c498047ba348614: especially to those who do not treat them with the respect they desire. Some of our number refused to accept this ruling by our council however.
textByHash.99bd02136fda02919b7644954ba8e2e76047493d: They began to mine these rocks, and set up transport systems to the various places of power that enchanted them.
textByHash.544435d075ddec55967b123563886c573edd140f: They even created temples at each of these places! This was going too far! We had no choice, but to expel them from our tribe forever!
textByHash.34e38474a0be2a2d0bb818617aeda4bfd078ed1f: We turned our backs upon them, and let them know they would never be welcome back to our tribes until they had released themselves from the rocks.
textByHash.5769580dacfac562d2ff011524e187597e490c52: This is the tale of how the outerlanders came about; through stealing from the gods, and from betraying our ideals. This is why we show them no trust.
textByHash.41345d083e976ac25d04d423e64902d861f844ad: Was there something else you wished to hear?
textByHash.478d60f2cb75fe3749f6d6bd64e137c4b1ca3592: Well let me know should you wish to hear our lore and histories. We value them highly.
textByHash.fb121570348152b581fa4badff988e7c4d43f8b8: Sorry, but your trail is not over quite yet.
textByHash.f0cb425daa8e6cb4cef0ea4d7b62d25085cbef2e: Find the answer to this question for me.
textByHash.2ba3fa01f7b19ac0b94e945a7cc6928e5bc24315: Please enter the answer to the question.
